topic.title: Thoughts on standardizing build backend support for non-standard targets

post.source_text:
What *frontend* tools do you expect to support this new interface? I don't expect pip to - pip currently only uses `build_wheel` and the associated methods, we don't even have a need for `build_sdist`.

Without a frontend using these new APIs, I don't see a good reason for standardising anything.

There's also the fact that I've seen almost no demand for building "non-standard targets" of the form you're describing, but that may simply because I don't work on tools that would see such demand. I note that the examples you found are *plugins* for hatch, and therefore could easily have been created to satisfy one person's nice use case (I don't *know* if that's the case, though, so I'm not claiming there's no demand, just that I'm not aware of any).

projected_parent_summary.summary: Matt proposes standardizing custom build targets in Python packaging, noting that only hatchling and setuptools currently support them. He outlines a potential interface, discusses pros and cons of adding a `targets` table to pyproject.toml, and seeks feedback on design choices, expressing uncertainty about the best approach.
projected_parent_summary.conversational_intent: proposing a standardization and seeking feedback
projected_parent_summary.important_uncertainty: Uncertainty about whether to include targets in pyproject.toml and how to name custom functions.

phrase_candidates.text: I feel like this overcomplicates the pyproject.toml
phrase_candidates.function: expressing uncertainty about the complexity of adding a targets table

phrase_candidates.text: I'm not sure whether or not the build frontend should support building targets that are not listed in the `targets` table.
phrase_candidates.function: expressing uncertainty about feature support for unlisted targets

phrase_candidates.text: I just wanted to get the ball rolling on something that I've wanted out of python packaging.
phrase_candidates.function: expressing desire to initiate discussion and gather feedback
